div.p: Step 1 — _begin_token() start = 0 token_start_line = 1 token_start_column = 1 Cursor: current = 0
Step 2 — advance() called in _scan_token() Reads source[0] → '>' current → 1 column → 2 ch = '>' dispatched to ch == '>' branch
Step 3 — _match('=') called peek() → source[1] = '=' '=' == '=' → TRUE advance() called: current → 2, column → 3 _match returns True
Step 4 — emit GREATER_EQUAL _current_lexeme() = source[0:2] = ">=" ✓ Token: GREATER_EQUAL(">=", line=1, col=1) Appended to self.tokens
Step 5 — _begin_token() start = 2 token_start_line = 1 token_start_column = 3 Cursor: current = 2
Step 6 — advance() called in _scan_token() Reads source[2] → '=' current → 3 column → 4 ch = '=' dispatched to ch == '=' branch
Step 7 — _match('=') called peek() → is_at_end() = True → returns '\0' '\0' != '=' → FALSE _match returns False cursor stays at current = 3
Step 8 — emit ASSIGN _current_lexeme() = source[2:3] = "=" ✓ Token: ASSIGN("=", line=1, col=3) Appended to self.tokens
is_at_end() = True → exit main loop Append Token(EOF, "", line=1, col=4)

If '>' stayed in SINGLE_CHAR_TOKENS: Iteration 1: ch='>' → dict lookup → emit GREATER(">") ← no lookahead! Iteration 2: ch='=' → _match('=') → TRUE → emit EQUAL_EQUAL("==") Iteration 3: (nothing left) Result: [GREATER, EQUAL_EQUAL] ✗ WRONG Root cause: Single-char dispatch fires before two-char lookahead has a chance to run. Fix: remove '>', '<', '=', '!' from SINGLE_CHAR_TOKENS and handle explicitly first.
after step3 current=2 start=0 consumed '=' lexeme=">="
after step7 current=3 start=2 peek='\0' lexeme="="
